questions.question: A 240-volt circuit supplies a 4,800-watt resistive load. What is the current draw?
questions.explanation: P = E x I; I = P / E = 4,800 / 240 = 20 amps. Ohm's law applied to power.

questions.question: What is the total resistance of three 30-ohm resistors connected in parallel?
questions.explanation: Parallel resistance: 1/Rt = 1/30 + 1/30 + 1/30 = 3/30; Rt = 10 ohms.

questions.question: If voltage remains constant and resistance doubles, current will:
questions.explanation: Ohm's law: I = E / R. If R doubles and E stays constant, I is halved.

questions.question: A circuit has a voltage of 120V and a current of 15A. What is the power consumed?
questions.explanation: P = E x I = 120 x 15 = 1,800 watts.

questions.question: In a pure resistive AC circuit, the power factor is:
questions.explanation: In a pure resistive circuit, voltage and current are in phase, so PF = 1.0.

questions.question: What is the unit of measurement for electrical conductance?
questions.explanation: Siemens (S) is the SI unit for electrical conductance, the reciprocal of ohms.

questions.question: A conductor carries 10 amps and has a resistance of 0.5 ohms. What is the voltage drop across the conductor?
questions.explanation: E = I x R = 10 x 0.5 = 5 volts.

questions.question: Which formula correctly expresses Ohm's law?
questions.explanation: Ohm's law: E (voltage) = I (current) x R (resistance).

questions.question: Two 12-volt batteries connected in series produce a total voltage of:
questions.explanation: Batteries in series: total voltage = 12 + 12 = 24 volts.

questions.question: In a series circuit with three resistors of 10, 20, and 30 ohms, the total resistance is:
questions.explanation: Series resistance: Rt = R1 + R2 + R3 = 10 + 20 + 30 = 60 ohms.

questions.question: Per NEC Article 210.8(A), GFCI protection is required in which of the following residential locations?
questions.explanation: NEC 210.8(A)(1) requires GFCI protection for all 125-volt, 15- and 20-amp receptacles in bathrooms.

questions.question: According to NEC 210.52(A), in a dwelling unit, no point along a wall line shall be more than ___ feet from a receptacle outlet.
questions.explanation: NEC 210.52(A)(1) -- no point along the floor line of any wall space shall be more than 6 feet from a receptacle outlet.

questions.question: NEC Article 314.16 governs:
questions.explanation: NEC 314.16 provides the rules for calculating box fill based on conductor count and size.

questions.question: Per NEC 240.4(D), the maximum overcurrent protection for 14 AWG copper conductors is:
questions.explanation: NEC 240.4(D)(3) -- 14 AWG copper: 15 amps maximum overcurrent protection.

questions.question: According to NEC Table 310.16, what is the ampacity of 8 AWG THWN copper conductor at 75 degrees C?
questions.explanation: NEC Table 310.16 lists 8 AWG THWN copper at 50 amps at 75 degrees C column.

questions.question: NEC 250.66 requires that the grounding electrode conductor for a 500 kcmil copper service entrance conductor be no smaller than:
questions.explanation: NEC Table 250.66 -- for service entrance conductors of 500 kcmil copper, the GEC must be at least 1/0 AWG copper.

questions.question: Per NEC 408.36, panelboards in other than dwelling units shall be provided with overcurrent protection having a rating of not greater than that of the:
questions.explanation: NEC 408.36 requires overcurrent protection not greater than the rating of the panelboard busbars.

questions.question: According to NEC 210.11(C)(3), at least one 20-amp branch circuit is required for:
questions.explanation: NEC 210.11(C)(3) requires at least one 20-amp branch circuit for bathroom receptacle outlets.

questions.question: NEC 300.5(A) requires that direct-buried UF cable under a residential driveway be installed at a minimum depth of:
questions.explanation: NEC Table 300.5 requires UF cable under a one- or two-family dwelling driveway to be buried at least 24 inches.

questions.question: Per NEC Table 220.12, the general lighting load for a dwelling unit is calculated at ___ VA per square foot.
questions.explanation: NEC Table 220.12 lists dwelling units at 3 VA per square foot for general lighting.

questions.question: EMT (electrical metallic tubing) is NOT permitted in which of the following locations?
choices: Where subject to severe physical damage
questions.explanation: NEC 358.12(1) -- EMT shall not be used where subject to severe physical damage.

questions.question: The maximum number of quarter bends (90 degrees) permitted between pull points for EMT is:
questions.explanation: NEC 358.26 -- the total number of bends shall not exceed 360 degrees (four 90-degree bends) between pull points.

questions.question: Type NM cable (Romex) is permitted in which of the following?
questions.explanation: NEC 334.10(1) -- Type NM cable is permitted in one- and two-family dwellings and their garages/storage buildings.

questions.question: When determining conduit fill, the maximum permitted fill for three or more conductors in a raceway is:
questions.explanation: NEC Table 1, Chapter 9 -- three or more conductors: 40% fill maximum.

questions.question: Flexible metal conduit (FMC) must be secured within ___ inches of each outlet box or fitting.
questions.explanation: NEC 348.30(A) -- FMC shall be securely fastened within 12 inches of each box, cabinet, or fitting.

questions.question: What is the minimum bending radius for trade size 1-inch EMT?
questions.explanation: NEC Table 344.24 (similar dimensions apply to EMT per 358.24) -- 1-inch trade size has a minimum bending radius of 6 inches.

questions.question: MC cable (metal-clad cable) must be supported at intervals not exceeding:
questions.explanation: NEC 330.30(B) -- MC cable shall be supported at intervals not exceeding 6 feet.

questions.question: Liquidtight flexible nonmetallic conduit (LFNC) is permitted in lengths not exceeding ___ feet when used for flexibility at equipment.
questions.explanation: NEC 356.10(7) -- LFNC is permitted in lengths not exceeding 6 feet where flexibility is necessary.

questions.question: Per NEC 312.6(A), conductors entering an enclosure shall not fill the wiring space at any cross section to more than ___ percent.
questions.explanation: NEC 312.6(A) -- conductors shall not fill the wiring space at any cross section to more than 40%.

questions.question: PVC conduit schedule 80 is required when the conduit is:
questions.explanation: NEC 352.10(F) -- Schedule 80 PVC is required where subject to physical damage.

questions.question: A 2,000 square foot dwelling unit has a general lighting load of:
questions.explanation: NEC Table 220.12: 2,000 sq ft x 3 VA/sq ft = 6,000 VA.

questions.question: Per NEC Table 220.42, the demand factor for the first 3,000 VA of general lighting in a dwelling is:
questions.explanation: NEC Table 220.42 -- the first 3,000 VA of general lighting load is at 100% demand.

questions.question: A commercial kitchen has four pieces of cooking equipment rated at 5 kW each. Using NEC Table 220.56, the demand factor for four units is 65%. What is the demand load?
questions.explanation: 4 units x 5,000 W = 20,000 W x 65% demand factor = 13,000 watts per NEC Table 220.56.

questions.question: What is the demand load for a 5,500-watt residential clothes dryer per NEC 220.54?
questions.explanation: NEC 220.54 -- a single dryer rated 5,500 W uses 5,000 W minimum or nameplate rating, whichever is larger: 5,500 W.

questions.question: A 200-amp, 120/240-volt single-phase service has a total calculated load of 38,400 VA. What is the service load in amps?
Per NEC 220.82, the optional calculation method allows the first 10 kVA of general loads at 100% and the remainder at:
questions.explanation: NEC 220.82(B) -- the optional method: first 10 kVA at 100%, remainder at 40%.

questions.question: A single-family dwelling has a 12 kW electric range. Per NEC Table 220.55, Column C, the demand load for one range not exceeding 12 kW is:
questions.explanation: NEC Table 220.55, Column C -- one range not over 12 kW: maximum demand 8 kW.

questions.question: What is the NEC-required minimum VA per small appliance branch circuit in a dwelling unit?
questions.explanation: NEC 210.11(C)(1) -- each small appliance branch circuit is rated at 1,500 VA.

questions.question: The minimum number of 20-amp small appliance branch circuits required for a dwelling kitchen is:
questions.explanation: NEC 210.11(C)(1) requires a minimum of two 20-amp small appliance branch circuits.

questions.question: A commercial building has a continuous lighting load of 50 amps. The overcurrent device must be rated at a minimum of:
questions.explanation: NEC 210.20(A) -- continuous loads require 125% sizing: 50 x 1.25 = 62.5 amps.

questions.question: Per NEC 250.122, the minimum equipment grounding conductor for a 60-amp circuit is:
questions.explanation: NEC Table 250.122 -- for a 60-amp circuit the minimum EGC is 10 AWG copper.

questions.question: The purpose of bonding is to:
questions.explanation: NEC 250.4(A)(3) -- bonding limits the voltage imposed on equipment enclosures during a fault.

questions.question: Per NEC 250.50, which of the following is required as a grounding electrode when present at a building?
questions.explanation: NEC 250.50/250.52(A)(1) -- a metal underground water pipe in contact with the earth for 10 feet or more is a required grounding electrode.

questions.question: The connection between the grounded conductor and the equipment grounding conductor at the service is called the:
questions.explanation: NEC 250.24(B)/250.28 -- the main bonding jumper connects the grounded conductor to the equipment ground at the service.

questions.question: Per NEC 250.52(A)(3), a concrete-encased electrode (Ufer ground) must contain at least ___ feet of bare 4 AWG copper conductor.
questions.explanation: NEC 250.52(A)(3) -- concrete-encased electrode: minimum 20 feet of bare 4 AWG copper.

questions.question: The grounding electrode conductor shall be connected to the grounding electrode by:
choices: Approved clamps, exothermic welding, or listed connectors
questions.explanation: NEC 250.70 -- connections to grounding electrodes shall be made by approved clamps, exothermic welding, or listed connectors.

questions.question: Per NEC 250.118, which of the following is NOT an acceptable equipment grounding conductor?
choices: Standard flexible metal conduit over 6 feet
questions.explanation: NEC 250.118(5) -- listed FMC is acceptable as an EGC only where the length does not exceed 6 feet.

questions.question: Per NEC 430.52, the maximum branch-circuit short-circuit and ground-fault protective device for a continuous-duty AC motor using an inverse-time circuit breaker is ___ percent of the motor FLC.
questions.explanation: NEC Table 430.52 -- inverse-time breaker for AC motor: 250% of motor FLC maximum.

questions.question: Per NEC Table 430.248, the full-load current of a 5 HP, 230-volt, single-phase motor is:
questions.explanation: NEC Table 430.248 -- 5 HP, 230V, single-phase motor FLC = 28 amps.

questions.question: Motor branch-circuit conductors must have an ampacity of not less than ___ percent of the motor full-load current.
questions.explanation: NEC 430.22(A) -- branch-circuit conductors must be at least 125% of motor FLC.

questions.question: When sizing the overload protection for a motor, NEC 430.32 requires the overload device to trip at not more than ___ percent of the motor nameplate FLA for motors with a service factor of 1.15 or greater.
questions.explanation: NEC 430.32(A)(1) -- overload device trips at not more than 125% of nameplate FLA for motors with SF >= 1.15.

questions.question: A motor disconnect must be within sight of the motor and be rated at least ___ percent of the motor full-load current.
questions.explanation: NEC 430.110(A) -- the disconnecting means must be rated at least 115% of the motor FLC.

questions.question: Per NEC 430.24, conductors supplying two or more motors shall have an ampacity not less than ___ percent of the largest motor FLC plus the sum of the remaining motor FLCs.
questions.explanation: NEC 430.24 -- 125% of the largest motor FLC plus the sum of the FLC of all other motors.

questions.question: A GFCI device is designed to trip when ground-fault current exceeds:
questions.explanation: NEC 210.8 / UL 943 -- GFCI devices trip at 4 to 6 milliamps of ground-fault current.

questions.question: Per NEC 680.26, the equipotential bonding grid for a permanently installed swimming pool must extend ___ feet horizontally beyond the inside walls of the pool.
questions.explanation: NEC 680.26(B)(2) -- the bonding grid extends 3 feet horizontally beyond the inside walls of the pool.

questions.question: NEC Article 517 covers electrical installations in:
questions.explanation: NEC Article 517 covers electrical installations in healthcare facilities.

questions.question: Arc-fault circuit interrupter (AFCI) protection is required in which of the following dwelling rooms per NEC 210.12(A)?
questions.explanation: NEC 210.12(A) lists bedrooms among the rooms requiring AFCI protection in dwelling units.

questions.question: Per NEC 422.11(E)(3), the branch-circuit rating for a household electric range rated at 8-3/4 kW or more shall be not less than:
questions.explanation: NEC 422.11(E)(3) -- household ranges rated 8-3/4 kW or more: minimum 40-amp branch circuit.

questions.question: NEC 410.116(B) requires that recessed luminaires installed in insulated ceilings be rated as:
questions.explanation: NEC 410.116(B) -- recessed luminaires in contact with insulation must be IC-rated (insulation contact).

questions.question: Per NEC 424.3(B), fixed electric space-heating equipment shall be considered a ___ load.
questions.explanation: NEC 424.3(B) -- fixed electric space-heating equipment shall be considered a continuous load.

questions.question: A 120-volt circuit has a resistance of 8 ohms. What is the current?
questions.explanation: I = E / R = 120 / 8 = 15 amps. Ohm's law.

questions.question: What is the total capacitance of two 20-microfarad capacitors connected in series?
questions.explanation: Series capacitance: 1/Ct = 1/20 + 1/20 = 2/20; Ct = 10 microfarads.

questions.question: A 10 HP motor operates at 85% efficiency. What is the input power in watts? (1 HP = 746 watts)
questions.explanation: Input = Output / Efficiency = (10 x 746) / 0.85 = 8,776 watts.

questions.question: In an AC circuit, apparent power is measured in:
questions.explanation: Apparent power = voltage x current and is measured in volt-amps (VA).

questions.question: What is the frequency of a four-pole generator turning at 1,800 RPM?
If 15 amps flows through a 4-ohm resistor, how much power is dissipated?
A transformer has a turns ratio of 10:1. If the primary voltage is 4,800 volts, the secondary voltage is:
questions.explanation: Secondary voltage = Primary / turns ratio = 4,800 / 10 = 480 volts.

questions.question: Three 60-ohm resistors connected in parallel have an equivalent resistance of:
The power factor in an inductive circuit can be improved by adding:
questions.explanation: Capacitance opposes inductance, improving the power factor in an inductive circuit.

questions.question: In a 3-phase system, the line voltage is 1.732 (square root of 3) times the phase voltage in which configuration?
questions.explanation: In a wye configuration, V-line = 1.732 x V-phase.

questions.question: Per NEC 210.8(A), GFCI protection is required for 125-volt, 15- and 20-amp receptacles in dwelling unit:
questions.explanation: NEC 210.8(A)(6) -- GFCI protection is required for receptacles serving dwelling kitchen countertops.

questions.question: NEC 230.79 requires the minimum service rating for a single-family dwelling to be:
questions.explanation: NEC 230.79(C) -- minimum 100-amp service for a single-family dwelling.

questions.question: Per NEC Table 110.26(A)(1), the minimum clear working space in front of electrical equipment rated 120-277 volts (Condition 1) is:
questions.explanation: NEC Table 110.26(A)(1) -- Condition 1, 120-277V: minimum 3 feet of clear working space.

questions.question: NEC 240.4(D) states that 12 AWG copper conductors shall be protected at not more than:
questions.explanation: NEC 240.4(D)(5) -- 12 AWG copper: 20 amps maximum overcurrent protection.

questions.question: Per NEC 210.52(C)(1), countertop receptacles in a dwelling kitchen must be installed so that no point along the wall line is more than ___ inches from a receptacle.
questions.explanation: NEC 210.52(C)(1) -- no point along the countertop wall line shall be more than 24 inches (2 feet) from a receptacle.

questions.question: NEC 110.14(C)(1) requires that terminations and conductors for circuits rated 100 amps or less be rated for:
questions.explanation: NEC 110.14(C)(1) -- circuits 100A or less: terminations rated for 60 or 75 degrees C (use lower for ampacity).

questions.question: Per NEC 230.70(A)(1), the service disconnecting means shall be installed at a:
choices: Readily accessible location nearest the point of service entrance
Location approved by the AHJ only
questions.explanation: NEC 230.70(A)(1) -- the service disconnect must be installed at a readily accessible location nearest the point of entrance of service conductors.

questions.question: NEC 408.40 requires individual grounding terminal bars in panelboards to be:
questions.explanation: NEC 408.40 -- equipment grounding terminal bars in panelboards shall be bonded to the cabinet or enclosure.

questions.question: Per NEC 210.52(E)(1), a dwelling unit requires at least one receptacle outlet for the:
questions.explanation: NEC 210.52(E)(1) -- outdoor receptacles: at least one front and one back, accessible at grade level.

questions.question: NEC 404.14(B)(1) requires that snap switches controlling inductive loads rated more than 80% of the switch rating be rated for:
questions.explanation: NEC 404.14(B)(1) -- AC general-use snap switches on inductive loads above 80% must be T-rated.

questions.question: Type AC cable (armored cable) shall be secured at intervals not exceeding:
questions.explanation: NEC 320.30(B) -- Type AC cable shall be secured at intervals not exceeding 4.5 feet.

questions.question: Per NEC 334.80, the ampacity of NM cable shall be determined after applying the adjustment factors of NEC 310.15(B)(3)(a) when more than ___ current-carrying conductors are bundled.
questions.explanation: NEC 334.80 -- adjustment factors apply when more than 2 NM cables (containing 2 or more current-carrying conductors) pass through the same opening.

questions.question: Rigid metal conduit (RMC) must be supported at intervals not exceeding:
questions.explanation: NEC 344.30(B)(2) -- RMC shall be supported at intervals not exceeding 10 feet.

questions.question: Per NEC Chapter 9, Table 1, what is the maximum conduit fill percentage for one conductor?
questions.explanation: NEC Chapter 9, Table 1 -- one conductor: 53% fill maximum.

questions.question: When running NM cable through holes in wooden studs, the edge of the hole must be at least ___ inch(es) from the nearest edge of the stud.
questions.explanation: NEC 334.108 -- holes in wood framing must be 1-1/4 inches from the nearest edge of the stud.

questions.question: Intermediate metal conduit (IMC) is permitted in:
questions.explanation: NEC 342.10 -- IMC is permitted in all atmospheric conditions and occupancies.

questions.question: Per NEC 376.22(B), the maximum number of conductors in a wireways shall not exceed ___ percent of the interior cross-sectional area.
questions.explanation: NEC 376.22(B) -- conductors shall not exceed 20% of the interior cross-sectional area of a wireway for splicing.

questions.question: Type UF cable installed as a substitute for NM cable must comply with all provisions of NEC Article:
questions.explanation: NEC 340.10(4) -- when Type UF cable is used as a substitute for NM cable it must comply with Article 334.

questions.question: Per NEC 314.16(B)(1), each 14 AWG conductor in a box fill calculation counts as ___ cubic inches.
questions.explanation: NEC Table 314.16(B) -- each 14 AWG conductor counts as 2.00 cubic inches.

questions.question: NEC 334.15(B) requires that NM cable installed in accessible attics be protected by guard strips when run across the top of floor joists within ___ feet of the attic access.
questions.explanation: NEC 334.23 -- NM cable in accessible attics: guard strips required within 6 feet of the scuttle hole or access opening.

questions.question: A 3,000 square foot dwelling unit has a general lighting load of:
questions.explanation: NEC Table 220.12: 3,000 sq ft x 3 VA/sq ft = 9,000 VA.

questions.question: Per NEC 220.14(J), each dwelling unit receptacle outlet is calculated at:
questions.explanation: NEC 220.14(J) -- each general-use receptacle outlet: 180 VA.

questions.question: A 10 kW, 240-volt single-phase electric water heater draws how many amps?

Per NEC 220.54, the demand load for five residential clothes dryers each rated at 5 kW is calculated using the nameplate rating times a demand factor from Table 220.54. For 5 dryers the demand factor is:
questions.explanation: NEC Table 220.54 -- for 5 dryers the demand factor is 80%.

questions.question: A 120/208-volt, 3-phase panel has a total connected load of 36,000 VA. What is the per-phase current?
For a multifamily dwelling with 10 units, NEC Table 220.42 permits a demand factor of ___ for general lighting loads beyond 120,000 VA.
questions.explanation: NEC Table 220.42 -- over 120,000 VA: 25% demand factor for general lighting in multifamily dwellings.

questions.question: A commercial building has 15,000 square feet of office space. Per NEC Table 220.12, the lighting load is ___ VA per square foot for office buildings.
questions.explanation: NEC Table 220.12 -- office buildings: 3.5 VA per square foot.

questions.question: NEC 220.61 allows a demand factor of ___ percent for the neutral load of a dryer.
questions.explanation: NEC 220.61(B) -- 70% demand factor for the neutral load of an electric range, dryer, and similar loads.

questions.question: The NEC-required laundry circuit load for a dwelling unit calculation is:
questions.explanation: NEC 220.52(B) -- laundry branch circuit load: 1,500 VA.

questions.question: A 150-amp feeder supplies a continuous load. The minimum conductor ampacity required is:
questions.explanation: NEC 215.2(A)(1) / 210.20(A) -- continuous loads: 150 x 1.25 = 187.5 amps minimum conductor ampacity.

questions.question: Per NEC Table 250.66, the grounding electrode conductor for a 2/0 AWG copper service entrance conductor is:
questions.explanation: NEC Table 250.66 -- for 2/0 AWG copper service entrance conductors, the GEC is 4 AWG copper.

questions.question: NEC 250.30(A) covers grounding of:
questions.explanation: NEC 250.30(A) covers grounding and bonding of separately derived systems such as transformers.

questions.question: Per NEC 250.52(A)(2), the ground rod must be driven to a minimum depth of:
questions.explanation: NEC 250.52(A)(5) -- ground rods must be driven to a minimum of 8 feet in contact with the earth.

questions.question: NEC 250.53(A)(2) requires that a single ground rod that does not have a resistance to ground of 25 ohms or less shall be supplemented by:
questions.explanation: NEC 250.53(A)(2) -- if a single rod does not meet 25 ohms, an additional electrode is required.

questions.question: Per NEC 250.64(B), the grounding electrode conductor shall be:
choices: Installed without splice or joint except as permitted
questions.explanation: NEC 250.64(C) -- the GEC shall be installed without splice or joint, except where permitted by listed irreversible connectors or exothermic welding.

questions.question: The minimum size equipment grounding conductor for a 200-amp circuit per NEC Table 250.122 is:
questions.explanation: NEC Table 250.122 -- for a 200-amp circuit, the minimum EGC is 6 AWG copper.

questions.question: NEC 250.104(A) requires that interior metal water piping be bonded to the:
questions.explanation: NEC 250.104(A) -- interior metal water piping shall be bonded to the service equipment enclosure, the grounded conductor at the service, the GEC, or the grounding electrode.

questions.question: Per NEC Table 430.250, the full-load current of a 10 HP, 460-volt, 3-phase motor is:
questions.explanation: NEC Table 430.250 -- 10 HP, 460V, 3-phase motor FLC = 14 amps.

questions.question: NEC 430.6(A)(1) requires motor branch-circuit sizing be based on:
questions.explanation: NEC 430.6(A)(1) -- motor branch-circuit sizing is based on FLC values from NEC tables, not the motor nameplate.

questions.question: Per NEC 430.52, the maximum dual-element fuse size for a single-phase AC motor is ___ percent of FLC.
questions.explanation: NEC Table 430.52 -- dual-element (time-delay) fuse: maximum 225% of motor FLC for AC motors.

questions.question: A motor controller must have a horsepower rating not lower than that of the motor, per NEC:
questions.explanation: NEC 430.83 -- the motor controller must have a horsepower rating not lower than that of the motor.

questions.question: Per NEC 430.109, a motor disconnecting means for a motor larger than 100 HP must be:
choices: A circuit breaker or molded case switch only
questions.explanation: NEC 430.109 -- for motors over 100 HP, the disconnect must be a circuit breaker or listed molded case switch.

questions.question: NEC 430.32(C) requires that overload protection for a motor NOT automatically restart the motor unless:
choices: The motor is less than 1 HP
A time delay relay is installed
questions.explanation: NEC 430.32(C) -- automatic restarting is permitted only where it will not be hazardous to persons.

questions.question: Per NEC 680.22(A)(5), receptacles located between 6 and 20 feet from the inside wall of a pool must be:
questions.explanation: NEC 680.22(A)(5) -- receptacles 6 to 20 feet from pool: GFCI protection required.

questions.question: NEC 501.10(A) requires wiring in Class I, Division 1 hazardous locations to be:
choices: Rigid metal conduit, IMC, or MI cable
questions.explanation: NEC 501.10(A) -- Class I, Division 1: threaded RMC, threaded IMC, or Type MI cable required.

questions.question: Per NEC 700.12, emergency systems must have power restored within ___ seconds of loss of normal power.
questions.explanation: NEC 700.12 -- emergency power must be available within 10 seconds of failure of normal supply.

questions.question: NEC 625.40 requires that electric vehicle supply equipment (EVSE) be supplied by a dedicated:
questions.explanation: NEC 625.40 -- EVSE shall be supplied by a dedicated branch circuit.

questions.question: Per NEC 690.12, a photovoltaic rapid shutdown system must reduce conductors to ___ volts or less within 30 seconds.
questions.explanation: NEC 690.12(B)(2) -- PV rapid shutdown: controlled conductors must be reduced to 30 volts or less within 30 seconds.

questions.question: NEC 480.9(A) requires that batteries in a battery room have a minimum clearance of ___ between battery terminals and any grounded surface.
questions.explanation: NEC 480.9(A) -- minimum of 6 inches clearance between battery terminals and grounded surfaces.

questions.question: Per NEC 706.7, energy storage systems (ESS) rated over ___ kWh require a disconnecting means.
questions.explanation: NEC 706.7 -- all energy storage systems require a disconnecting means regardless of size.

questions.question: A 480-volt, 3-phase motor draws 20 amps. What is the apparent power (VA)?
questions.explanation: 3-phase apparent power: S = E x I x 1.732 = 480 x 20 x 1.732 = 16,627 VA.

questions.question: What is the total resistance when a 10-ohm and a 15-ohm resistor are connected in parallel?
A conductor 500 feet long has a resistance of 0.2 ohms per 1,000 feet. With 30 amps flowing, what is the voltage drop for the round trip?
questions.explanation: R total = 2 x (500/1000) x 0.2 = 0.2 ohms round trip. Vd = I x R = 30 x 0.2 = 6 volts.

questions.question: In a purely capacitive AC circuit, the current:
choices: Is in phase with the voltage
questions.explanation: In a purely capacitive circuit, current leads voltage by 90 degrees.

questions.question: A 208-volt, single-phase heater draws 24 amps. What is the wattage?
questions.explanation: P = E x I = 208 x 24 = 4,992 watts for a single-phase resistive load.

questions.question: What is the inductive reactance of a coil with an inductance of 0.05 henrys at 60 Hz? (XL = 2 x pi x f x L)
A 120-volt circuit has a load at the end of a run that draws 16 amps. If the maximum voltage drop allowed is 3%, the maximum voltage drop in volts is:
questions.explanation: 3% of 120V = 0.03 x 120 = 3.6 volts maximum voltage drop.

questions.question: The unit of measurement for inductance is the:
questions.explanation: The henry (H) is the SI unit for inductance.

questions.question: A 240/120-volt, single-phase transformer is rated at 10 kVA. What is the maximum full-load current on the 240-volt side?
The impedance of a series RL circuit with R = 30 ohms and XL = 40 ohms is:
questions.explanation: Z = square root of (R^2 + XL^2) = square root of (900 + 1600) = square root of 2500 = 50 ohms.

questions.question: Per NEC 210.8(F), GFCI protection is required for all 250-volt receptacles rated ___ amps or less in non-dwelling locations.
questions.explanation: NEC 210.8(F) -- GFCI protection required for 250-volt, 50-amp or less receptacles in non-dwelling locations.

questions.question: NEC 110.26(A)(3) requires a minimum headroom of ___ feet in the working space around electrical equipment.
questions.explanation: NEC 110.26(A)(3) -- minimum headroom of 6.5 feet (6 feet 6 inches) in the working space.

questions.question: Per NEC Table 310.16, the ampacity of 3 AWG THHN copper conductor at 90 degrees C is:
questions.explanation: NEC Table 310.16 -- 3 AWG THHN copper at 90 degrees C column: 110 amps.

questions.question: NEC 210.52(G)(1) requires a receptacle outlet within ___ feet of the intended location of a gas-fired appliance in a dwelling garage.
questions.explanation: NEC 210.52(G)(1) -- receptacle outlet within 6 feet of the intended location of each appliance in the garage.

questions.question: Per NEC 240.6(A), which of the following is a standard size overcurrent device?
questions.explanation: NEC 240.6(A) -- standard sizes include 80 amps. 45, 65, and 120 are not standard.

questions.question: NEC 230.24(B)(1) requires that service-drop conductors over residential driveways maintain a minimum clearance of:
questions.explanation: NEC 230.24(B)(1) -- service drops over residential property and driveways: minimum 12 feet clearance.

questions.question: Per NEC 422.12, central heating equipment (other than fixed electric space-heating) shall be supplied by a branch circuit rated not less than ___ percent of the nameplate rating.
questions.explanation: NEC 422.12 -- central heating: branch circuit rated not less than 125% of nameplate, as it is a continuous load.

questions.question: NEC 210.70(A)(2)(b) requires that hallways of ___ feet or more in length in dwelling units have at least one lighting outlet controlled by a wall switch.
questions.explanation: NEC 210.70(A)(2)(b) -- hallways 10 feet or more in length require a wall switch-controlled lighting outlet.

questions.question: Per NEC 338.10(B)(4)(a), the ampacity of Type SE cable used as interior wiring without all conductors insulated must be determined based on the ___ degree C column of Table 310.16.
questions.explanation: NEC 338.10(B)(4)(a) -- SE cable without all conductors insulated uses the 60 degrees C column of Table 310.16.

questions.question: NEC 406.12 requires tamper-resistant receptacles in which locations?
choices: All dwelling unit locations specified in 210.52
questions.explanation: NEC 406.12 -- tamper-resistant receptacles required in all dwelling unit locations specified in 210.52.

questions.question: Per NEC 352.44, PVC conduit expansion fittings are required when the length change due to thermal expansion exceeds:
questions.explanation: NEC 352.44 -- expansion fittings required where expansion/contraction exceeds 3/4 inch.

questions.question: NM cable passing through a metal plate (nail plate) to protect against nail or screw penetration must have a plate at least ___ inch thick.
questions.explanation: NEC 334.108 / 300.4(A)(1) -- nail plates (steel plates) must be at least 1/16 inch thick.

questions.question: Per NEC 300.4(G), cables and raceways installed parallel to framing members must be protected by a steel plate where the member is less than ___ inches from the nearest edge.
questions.explanation: NEC 300.4(D) -- cables parallel to framing members: protected by a steel plate where less than 1-1/4 inches from the edge.

questions.question: The maximum distance between supports for 1/2-inch EMT is:
questions.explanation: NEC Table 358.30(A) -- 1/2-inch and 3/4-inch EMT: maximum 10 feet between supports.

questions.question: Per NEC Chapter 9, Table 4, the internal area of a 1-inch EMT conduit is approximately:
questions.explanation: NEC Chapter 9, Table 4 -- 1-inch EMT has an internal area of approximately 0.864 square inches.

questions.question: NEC 320.15 requires that Type AC cable be installed with a minimum bending radius of ___ times the diameter of the cable.
questions.explanation: NEC 320.24 -- Type AC cable: bending radius not less than 7 times the external diameter of the cable.

questions.question: Per NEC 356.30, liquidtight flexible nonmetallic conduit (LFNC) must be secured at intervals not exceeding:
questions.explanation: NEC 356.30 -- LFNC secured at intervals not exceeding 3 feet.

questions.question: The maximum total degrees of bends permitted between pull points in RMC is:
questions.explanation: NEC 344.26 -- RMC bends shall not exceed 360 degrees total between pull points.

questions.question: Per NEC 314.28(A)(1), in a straight pull, the minimum length of a pull box must be ___ times the trade size of the largest raceway.
questions.explanation: NEC 314.28(A)(1) -- straight pulls: box length at least 8 times the trade size of the largest raceway.

questions.question: NEC 362.10 permits electrical nonmetallic tubing (ENT) in which of the following?
choices: Concealed within walls of buildings not exceeding 3 stories
questions.explanation: NEC 362.10(1) -- ENT permitted concealed within walls, floors, and ceilings of buildings not exceeding 3 stories.

questions.question: A 1,500 square foot dwelling with two small appliance circuits and one laundry circuit has a total general loads VA (before demand) of:
questions.explanation: General lighting: 1,500 x 3 = 4,500 VA. Small appliance: 2 x 1,500 = 3,000 VA. Laundry: 1,500 VA. Total = 9,000 VA.

questions.question: Per NEC 220.55, Column C, three household electric ranges rated at 12 kW each have a maximum demand of:
questions.explanation: NEC Table 220.55, Column C -- 3 ranges, each not over 12 kW: maximum demand = 14 kW.

questions.question: A 240-volt single-phase air conditioning unit draws 32 amps. The minimum branch-circuit conductor ampacity is:
questions.explanation: NEC 440.32 -- single motor-compressor: conductor ampacity not less than 125% of rated-load current = 32 x 1.25 = 40 amps.

questions.question: Per NEC 220.82 (optional method), a dwelling has a total connected load of 45 kVA. The first 10 kVA is at 100%. The remaining 35 kVA is at 40%. The total demand load is:
The NEC requires how many small appliance branch circuits for a dwelling unit?
questions.explanation: NEC 210.11(C)(1) -- a minimum of 2 small appliance branch circuits required.

questions.question: A 4,500-watt, 240-volt electric water heater is considered a continuous load. The minimum circuit ampacity is:
Per NEC Table 220.55, Note 1, if a range is rated over 12 kW, the Column C value increases by ___ percent for each kW (or fraction) over 12 kW.
questions.explanation: NEC Table 220.55, Note 1 -- increase Column C value by 5% for each kW or fraction exceeding 12 kW.

questions.question: A commercial building with 20,000 sq ft of warehouse space has a lighting load of ___ VA per NEC Table 220.12 (warehouse, storage).
questions.explanation: NEC Table 220.12 -- warehouse/storage: 0.25 VA per sq ft. 20,000 x 0.25 = 5,000 VA.

questions.question: Per NEC 220.14(K), each bank of multioutlet assembly counted as a receptacle load in non-industrial applications uses ___ VA per 5-foot section.
questions.explanation: NEC 220.14(K) -- each 5-foot section of multioutlet assembly in non-industrial applications: 180 VA x 5 = 900 VA.

questions.question: The amperage for a 120/240-volt single-phase service with a total demand of 24,000 VA is:
Per NEC 250.122(B), when ungrounded conductors are increased in size for voltage drop, the equipment grounding conductor must:
questions.explanation: NEC 250.122(B) -- when conductors are upsized for voltage drop, the EGC must be increased proportionally.

questions.question: NEC 250.52(A)(7) requires a ground ring to be buried at a depth of at least:
questions.explanation: NEC 250.52(A)(7) -- the ground ring must encircle the building at a depth of at least 30 inches.

questions.question: Per NEC 250.20(B), AC systems of 50 to 1,000 volts shall be grounded when the system can be grounded so that the maximum voltage to ground does not exceed:
questions.explanation: NEC 250.20(B)(1) -- the system shall be grounded where the maximum voltage to ground does not exceed 150 volts.

questions.question: Per NEC 250.118(6)(d), liquidtight flexible metal conduit (LFMC) is acceptable as an EGC in trade sizes up to:
questions.explanation: NEC 250.118(6)(d) -- LFMC is an acceptable EGC in trade sizes 3/8 through 1-1/4 inches.

questions.question: NEC 250.64(E) requires that the grounding electrode conductor be protected where exposed to severe physical damage by enclosing it in:
choices: RMC, IMC, PVC, or other approved means
questions.explanation: NEC 250.64(B) -- where exposed to severe physical damage, the GEC must be protected by RMC, IMC, Schedule 80 PVC, or other approved means.

questions.question: Per NEC Table 250.122, the minimum equipment grounding conductor for a 30-amp overcurrent device is:
questions.explanation: NEC Table 250.122 -- 30-amp overcurrent device: minimum 10 AWG copper EGC.

questions.question: NEC 250.24(A)(5) requires that the grounded conductor (neutral) be run to each service disconnecting means and bonded to the enclosure for services using:
questions.explanation: NEC 250.24(C) -- the grounded conductor (neutral) must be run to each service disconnecting means and bonded per the grounded conductor method.

questions.question: Per NEC Table 430.250, the full-load current for a 25 HP, 460-volt, 3-phase motor is:
questions.explanation: NEC Table 430.250 -- 25 HP, 460V, 3-phase motor FLC = 34 amps.

questions.question: The minimum conductor ampacity for a 40 HP, 460V, 3-phase motor (FLC = 52 amps per Table 430.250) is:
questions.explanation: NEC 430.22(A) -- 125% of 52 amps = 65 amps minimum conductor ampacity.

questions.question: Per NEC 430.52, what is the maximum instantaneous-trip (magnetic only) circuit breaker rating for an AC motor?
questions.explanation: NEC Table 430.52 -- instantaneous-trip (magnetic-only) breaker: maximum 800% of motor FLC (with permitted increase per NEC 430.52(C)(1)).

questions.question: A motor nameplate reads 460V, 3-phase, 30 HP, FLA 38 amps, SF 1.0. Per NEC 430.32, the maximum overload trip setting is:
questions.explanation: NEC 430.32(A)(1) -- for motors with SF less than 1.15 and no marked temperature rise, overload trips at 115% of nameplate FLA: 38 x 1.10 = 41.8 amps.

questions.question: Per NEC 430.53(D), a single branch circuit supplying a single motor-compressor shall have an ampacity not less than ___ percent of the rated-load current.
questions.explanation: NEC 440.32 -- single motor-compressor: conductor ampacity at least 125% of the rated-load current.

questions.question: NEC 430.102(B) requires that the motor disconnect be located:
questions.explanation: NEC 430.102(B) -- the motor disconnecting means must be in sight from the motor location and the driven machinery.

questions.question: Per NEC 210.12(A), AFCI protection is required for 120-volt, 15- and 20-amp branch circuits supplying outlets in dwelling unit:
choices: Kitchens, family rooms, bedrooms, and living rooms
questions.explanation: NEC 210.12(A) -- AFCI protection required for 120V, 15/20A circuits in kitchens, family rooms, dining rooms, living rooms, parlors, libraries, dens, bedrooms, sunrooms, recreation rooms, closets, hallways, laundry areas, and similar rooms.

questions.question: NEC 680.21(C) requires that a swimming pool pump motor have a branch-circuit GFCI protective device rated at:
questions.explanation: NEC 680.21(C) -- the GFCI device for a pool pump motor is the standard Class A type rated at 5 milliamps.

questions.question: Per NEC 502.10(A)(1), Class II, Division 1 locations permit wiring by:
choices: RMC or IMC with threaded fittings
questions.explanation: NEC 502.10(A)(1) -- Class II, Division 1: threaded RMC or threaded IMC with listed fittings.

questions.question: NEC 695.6(B) requires that fire pump supply conductors be protected against damage by being routed:
choices: Through the building using RMC, IMC, or listed electrical circuit protective systems
In NM cable with steel plates
questions.explanation: NEC 695.6(B) -- fire pump supply conductors routed through the building shall be in RMC, IMC, or listed electrical circuit protective systems.

questions.question: Per NEC 694.22, small wind electric systems rated over ___ volts DC shall comply with rapid shutdown requirements.
questions.explanation: NEC 694.22 -- small wind systems over 80 volts DC require rapid shutdown.

questions.question: NEC 440.12 requires that air conditioning disconnects be rated at least ___ percent of the nameplate rated-load current.
questions.explanation: NEC 440.12(B) -- the air conditioning disconnect rated at least 115% of the nameplate rated-load current or branch-circuit selection current, whichever is greater.

questions.question: Per NEC 230.42(A), service entrance conductors must have an ampacity not less than ___ percent of the continuous load plus 100% of the non-continuous load.
questions.explanation: NEC 230.42(A) -- service entrance conductors: 125% of continuous load plus 100% of non-continuous load.
